explain: to separate; to move in different directions
a line that someone or something moves on
to make (someone or something) uncertain or unable to understand something
the way in which an animal's color or shape matches its surroundings
to look like what is around you
to fit into a certain environment
to include or contain as a necessary part
the act of defending someone or something from attack
to cover a place or shower someone with drops of a liquid
statement: The crowd scattered when the police charged.
He drove in the direction of the farm.
The general was trying to confuse the enemy.
Rabbits use their white fur as camouflage in the snow.
The fish settles on the sandy ocean bottom where it blends in perfectly.
He has not yet adapted to the climate.
The school has evolved its own style of teaching.
He sprayed water on the flowers this morning.
